publisher: SaskMath — Indigenous Ways of Knowing in Mathematics
description: Western-Canada-grounded annotated list of vetted Indigenous math resources, frequently updated and aligned with the Saskatchewan and (transferable to) Ontario curriculum.

title: Science North Education — Grade 9 Earth and Space Resources
description: Engaging science activities and experiments meeting Ontario Science and Technology curriculum. Includes lessons like calculating helium balloons needed to reach the edge of space, available for academic and applied streams.

title: Science North — Grade 8 Lesson Plans Hub
description: Sudbury-based Science North's Ontario-aligned Grade 8 hub featuring a dedicated Cells lesson with classroom video, student handout, and offline lesson plan.

title: Scotiabank: Financial Literacy Hub
description: Collection of articles, videos, and tools covering banking basics, saving strategies, and smart spending habits. Canadian-focused content with relevant examples.

title: Smarter Than Cash: Digital Money
description: Explore the evolution from cash to digital payments. Students learn about debit cards, mobile payments, and online banking security while discussing the future of money.

title: StudyPug — Ontario Grade 9 Math Curriculum (MTH1W)
publisher: Open Courseware and Curriculum-Aligned Online Practice
description: Curriculum-mapped online lessons and practice problems for every MTH1W expectation, with short video walkthroughs, worked examples, and "try it" practice questions. Useful as student remediation or independent practice support.

title: Teacher Resource Cabin — Grade 9 Ontario Math MTH1W Full Semester Bundle
description: Full-semester bundle of six MTH1W units (SEL, Number, Algebra, Data, Geometry & Measurement, Financial Literacy) with workbooks, slide decks, quizzes, and modelling assignments. English and French versions available.

title: Teachers Pay Teachers — Ontario Grade 9 Earth and Space Resources
description: Marketplace of teacher-created resources including lesson plans, presentations, assessments, and activities aligned to Ontario Grade 9 curriculum. Many free resources available.

title: Teachers Pay Teachers — Stacey's Circle Grade 7 NEW Ontario Math FULL-YEAR Bundle
publisher: Teaching Units & Lesson Plans — TPT & Commercial
description: Alternative full-year Grade 7 Ontario resource with structured coding lessons — teachers pick the bundle that best matches their workflow.

title: TED-Ed — Out of This World Collection
description: Collection of animated TED-Ed lessons celebrating space and astronomy ideas, covering solar flares, black holes, asteroids, and the Big Bang with customizable classroom features.

title: The Indigenous Mathematics K–12 Network (UBC Faculty of Education)
description: Comprehensive resource portal from UBC's Indigenous Math Education Network, with lesson plans, symposium proceedings, and video talks from Indigenous educators. Resources are tagged by math content area so Grade 7–8 teachers can search by strand.

title: TheLearningExchange — Educator Resources for Mathematics
description: Ontario Ministry-affiliated educator portal with curated math resources including transformation demonstrations, coordinate grid tasks, and geometric reasoning video exemplars. Strong Ontario alignment with 2020 curriculum.

title: Transforming Shapes Collection
description: Complete collection exploring transformations informally and precisely, with activities focused on describing, classifying, and applying translations, reflections, and rotations.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 History and Geography Hub
description: TVO's Ontario-aligned hub for Grade 8 Geography learners and teachers, organized by strand with embedded video, articles, and student tasks tied to the 2018 curriculum.

title: TVO Learn — Mathematics Grades 7-8 (Geometry Unit)
description: TVO's high-quality video lessons and interactive activities aligned to Ontario curriculum, covering geometric reasoning with visual demonstrations.

title: United Nations — Sustainable Development Student Resources
description: UN portal of student-friendly explainers and downloadable kits on each SDG; pairs well with the strand's responses-to-global-inequalities expectation.

title: Upper Canada DSB Virtual Library — Grade 7 Structures
description: Upper Canada District School Board's curated LibGuide for Grade 7 Structures and Mechanisms, organized by topic with vetted videos, readings, and interactive resources.

title: Upper Canada Virtual Library — Algebra Grade 7 LibGuide
description: Digital library guide curating Grade 7 algebra resources including databases, videos, and websites aligned to Ontario curriculum expectations.

title: Upper Canada Virtual Library — Grade 7 New France & BNA LibGuide
description: Upper Canada District School Board's Strand-A-specific research portal listing vetted databases, ebooks, and websites for the 1713-1800 era.

title: Upper Canada Virtual Library — Grade 8 Financial Literacy LibGuide
description: Ontario public-board-curated LibGuide for Grade 8 financial literacy with vetted links to curriculum documents, videos, and practice resources.

title: Upper Canada Virtual Library — Grade 8 Math Number LibGuide
description: Upper Canada DSB's Grade 8 math LibGuide aggregating Ontario curriculum expectations, resource material, and homework help links for Number Sense and Operations, including board-vetted tutorials and practice sites.

title: Virtual Science Teachers — Earth Science Interactives
description: Collection of interactive simulations and virtual lab experiences designed for earth science instruction without expensive laboratory equipment requirements.

title: Visual Fractions — Online Calculators & Tools
description: Long-running site (since 1999) offering visual fraction calculators, percent-to-fraction tools, and step-by-step worked examples — useful as a student reference and for differentiating instruction.

title: World Geography Games
description: Interactive map games for learning countries, capitals, physical features, climates, and regions. Builds geographic literacy through gamified practice.

title: WRDSB MTH1W Course Resources (Waterloo Region DSB)
description: Comprehensive teacher-facing hub with course planning options (including spiralled approaches), a rich tasks database, Thinking Classroom tasks, and the first-10-days plan for MTH1W.

title: You are the Economy
description: You Are the Economy is a set of six lessons and activities that explore economic and financial literacy and are designed to fit within social studies, history, geography, economics, business and career courses

title: 2 Peas and a Dog — Grade 7 History Strand A Unit (New France & BNA 1713-1800)
description: Comprehensive Ontario-curriculum-aligned 24-lesson unit including readings, audio, slideshows, and assessment rubrics; explicitly mapped to the 2018 (and 2023 update) expectations.

title: 3Blue1Brown - Mathematics Visualization Channel
description: Professionally animated explanations of mathematical concepts emphasizing visual intuition; excellent for showing how different strands interconnect.

title: 7e année — Mathématiques (Curriculum Ontario)
publisher: Official Ontario Curriculum Documents in French
description: Grade 7 expectations and learning content in French, with hyperlinked navigation across the five strands. The default reference document for Ontario French-language Grade 7 math teachers.

title: Actively Learn: Interactive Reading
description: Platform for assigning texts with embedded questions, notes, and media. Students engage with texts through annotations and discussions.

title: AFEMO — Ressources et formations
publisher: French Pedagogical Guides and Teacher Resources
description: AFEMO (Association francophone pour l'enseignement des mathématiques en Ontario) hub with pedagogy articles on growth mindset, error analysis, technology integration, and curriculum implementation in French Ontario contexts.

title: Allen Institute — 3D Cell Viewer
description: Browser-based 3D viewer of nearly 25,000 individual real human cells from the Allen Institute. Students can rotate, zoom, and adjust contrast on real microscopy data — an authentic enrichment beyond textbook diagrams.

title: Bank of Canada Museum website
description: The Bank of Canada Museum website features a number of activities and articles appropriate for Children 10+ related to the history of money in Canada, the Gold Standard and coins.

title: Bank of Canada Museum — Activities and Games
description: Interactive money-themed games and printable activity sheets that can be used as warm-ups, stations, or independent practice for F1 expectations.

title: Begbie Contest Society — Canadian Primary Sources
description: Curated collection of 19th-century Canadian political cartoons, photographs, and posters (1850-present) with critical-thinking prompts — invaluable for A2 perspective-analysis and document-based questions.

title: Brilliant Labs / Labos Créatifs — Maker-Centered Learning
description: Atlantic Canadian charity providing free maker-space and coding classroom resources, with grade 7 as its explicit on-ramp cohort for structured coding projects.

title: Calcul@TICE
publisher: Mental Math, Calcul Mental & Game-based Practice
description: Mental-math practice platform from Académie de Lille with eight Rallyes covering CP through 3e — useful warm-up resource for Ontario Grade 7 number-sense fluency, with offline mobile app.

title: Canadian Geographic Education — Indigenous Peoples Atlas of Canada Hub
publisher: Indigenous Perspectives on Land and Settlement
description: Canadian Geographic Education's classroom companion to the Indigenous Peoples Atlas, with downloadable lesson plans, inquiry activities, and discussion guides aligned to Grade 7-12 social studies and geography expectations.

title: Canadian Museum of History: Learning Resources
description: Educational resources exploring Canadian history, Indigenous heritage, and national identity. Includes virtual tours, primary sources, and lesson plans.

title: Canadian Museum of Immigration at Pier 21
description: Canada's national museum of immigration providing 2,000+ digitized stories, oral histories, archival images, and teacher lesson plans on Canadian immigration from 1928 onward — strong real-voices source for case studies.

title: CELLS alive! — Interactive Cell Cycle and Cell Models
description: Long-running cell-biology learning resource with step-through interactive animations of the cell cycle, plant and animal cell anatomy, and printable worksheets. A reliable supplement for the cell-division portion of B2.

title: CEMC Computer Science Circles — Python from Scratch (University of Waterloo)
description: Free Python-from-scratch course from the University of Waterloo's CEMC. Auto-graded exercises, code visualizations, and a "guru" feature so a teacher can monitor a student's progress. Excellent transition tool for Grade 8 → Grade 9 (MTH1W).

title: CEMC Courseware – Python from Scratch
description: University of Waterloo's beginner-friendly Python course that teaches programming concepts without assuming prior knowledge, with mathematical applications woven throughout. No registration required.

title: CEMC Waterloo — Grade 7/8 Mathematics Courseware
description: University of Waterloo's CEMC free open courseware generally following the Ontario curriculum for Grades 7 and 8. Includes modules on geometry, measurement (including circles and 3D figures), and the Pythagorean relationship, with concept checks and immediate feedback quizzes.

title: CEMC – Problem of the Week (Data & Probability Problems)
description: Weekly math problems from the Centre for Education in Mathematics and Computing, grouped by strand and difficulty. Includes rich data-based problems suitable for Grade 7–8 enrichment and classroom warm-ups.

title: CEPEO — Cycle intermédiaire (7e, 8e année)
publisher: Ontario French-Language Boards & Provincial PD Networks
description: Conseil des écoles publiques de l'Est de l'Ontario (CEPEO) intermediate cycle hub, curating French-language mathematics teaching resources, transition supports, and parent guides for Grade 7 and 8.

title: CIVIX: Student Vote and Civic Education
description: Canada's leading civic education organization. Resources on democracy, elections, government structure, and active citizenship for young Canadians.

title: Code for Fun — Middle School Lesson Plans & Resources
description: Free resource library with middle school coding lesson plans emphasizing problem-solving and algorithmic thinking; includes both introductory and enrichment activities suitable for Grade 7.

title: Code.org — Nested Loops (Course F Lesson 8)
description: Self-paced puzzle sequence that introduces nested loops — a core Grade 7 concept — with an accompanying teacher lesson plan, discussion prompts, and student assessment rubric.

title: Consumer Ed: Smart Shopping Skills
description: Lessons on consumer awareness including understanding advertising, comparing prices, and making informed purchasing decisions. Includes activities analyzing real ads and promotions.

title: CTA Math (TDSB Central Tech) — Grade 9 MTH1W Course Page
publisher: Ontario School Board and Teacher Hubs
description: Central Toronto Academy math department's MTH1W landing page providing semester pacing, unit packages, review handouts, and course expectations — a representative example of an urban TDSB-school MTH1W implementation.

title: Curriculum and Resources Portal — Grade 7 History (Strand A)
description: Ontario Ministry's official curriculum portal page giving overall and specific expectations for Grade 7 History Strand A with embedded teacher prompts and student response examples.

title: Cybersavoir CSSDM — Mathématique au secondaire (Ressources)
publisher: Quebec Francophone Platforms (used cross-border in Ontario classrooms)
description: Centre de services scolaire de Montréal's secondary-math resource hub: regularly updated curated links, digital task ideas, and integration tips suitable for adapting to Ontario intermediate classrooms.

title: Data Literacy Training
description: Data Literacy Training in a variety of formats, including text, activities and video. Intended for beginner adults but may be useful to advance Grade 9s.

title: Data Management Unit — Grade 7 Ontario (Teachers Pay Teachers)
description: Comprehensive NO-PREP Data Management unit with lessons, practice worksheets, interactive activities, math centres, and assessments covering Ontario expectations.

title: Desmos Classroom — Budgeting and Financial Decision-Making Activities
description: Interactive Desmos activities where students create and modify budgets, exploring how changes in income and expenses affect financial outcomes using interactive graphing tools.

title: Desmos Graphing Calculator
description: Free browser-based graphing calculator enabling students to graph functions, plot points, visualize algebraic equations, add sliders, and animate graphs. Teachers across the world use this tool to make lessons interactive and help students explore mathematical relationships.

title: Ditch That Textbook — Google CS First: Free Interactive Coding Lessons for Your Class
description: Widely read educator blog post demystifying the rollout of Google CS First in a middle-school classroom, with setup tips, classroom management, and differentiation ideas.

title: Ducks Unlimited Canada — Wetlands 101
description: Interactive site introducing wetland ecology, ecosystem services, and conservation, ideal for natural-resource sustainability lessons in the Grade 7 Geography classroom.

title: Enriched Academy — SmartStart (Junior High Financial Literacy)
description: SmartStart is Enriched Academy's junior-high financial literacy program — five 30–40 minute modules with videos, levelled lesson plans, and reflection prompts. Canadian curriculum alignment and well-suited to Grade 8 Strand F.

title: EQAO Grade 9 Assessment Framework
description: The official assessment blueprint showing the distribution of questions across MTH1W strands. Grade 7–8 teachers use this to understand item difficulty and cognitive demand students will encounter in secondary.

title: EQAO — Grade 9 Assessment of Mathematics: Released Questions 2024
publisher: Official Ontario Curriculum and Ministry Pages
description: Most recent EQAO Grade 9 Assessment of Mathematics released questions package, including provincial response data, scoring guides, and student work samples — useful for diagnostic use and validating MTH1W programme alignment.

title: Esri ArcGIS StoryMaps Gallery
description: Esri ArcGIS StoryMaps platform for student-created interactive map narratives; the public gallery includes existing exemplar stories on global migration and sustainable cities suitable for jigsaw-style inquiry assignments.

title: ETFO Members Sharing in Assessment — Intermediate Rubrics
description: ETFO's curated bank of teacher-tested rubrics for intermediate math, organized by task type. A complement to the diagnostic/formative tools page already in the database.

title: ETFO — Choosing the Right Assessment Tool
description: ETFO's guide to selecting an appropriate assessment tool based on instructional purpose. Invaluable companion to the formative/diagnostic tasks repository for Ontario intermediate math teachers.

title: EVERFI: Financial Literacy Modules
description: Interactive digital courses covering banking, credit, investing, and financial planning. Gamified approach with assessments and teacher dashboards for progress monitoring.

title: FDIC Money Smart for Young People — Grades 6-8 Curriculum
description: Free 12-lesson FDIC Money Smart curriculum for Grades 6-8 including slides, educator guides, and student handouts; strong fit for Grade 8 F1.2/F1.3/F1.6 expectations (note: US examples — adapt currency references for Canadian context).

title: Financial Literacy Ontario (F1) – Grade 7 Unit (TPT)
description: An editable Grade 7 math unit plan for Ontario Strand F1 covering budgeting, interest rates, exchange rates, and factors affecting financial decisions, with lesson slides, practice activities, and assessments.

title: Fishtank Learning – Grade 7 Statistics Unit
description: A free, rigorous statistics unit where students investigate sampling to make inferences about larger populations. Includes hands-on activities for random sampling, calculations of mean and MAD, and comparing populations using sample data.

title: Fishtank Learning – Grade 8 Bivariate Data Unit
description: A free unit where Grade 8 students explore patterns of association in bivariate data, construct scatter plots, analyse two-way tables, and determine associations between categorical variables.

title: Fishtank Learning — 7th Grade Math: Equations & Inequalities Unit
description: Free Fishtank Learning unit with 14 lessons explicitly sequenced from concrete (tape diagrams) to abstract (algebraic manipulation), including diagnostic and exit tickets for every lesson.

title: Fishtank Learning — Grade 8 Geometry Unit (Measurement Focus)
publisher: Curated Collections & Enrichment (NRICH, Open Middle, Youcubed, Fishtank, CEMC)
description: Free, complete Grade 8 geometry unit with detailed lessons on Pythagorean theorem applications and 3D measurement of various solids.

title: Fishtank Learning — Grade 8 Unit: Solving One-Variable Equations
description: A complete unit with daily objectives, anticipated student thinking, problem sets (with answer keys), and pre/post assessments. Provides ready-to-teach lesson plans for the heart of Ontario C2.3.

title: Flip: Video Discussion Platform
description: Students record and share short video responses to prompts. Builds oral communication skills and creates inclusive classroom discussions.

title: Friday Math — Ontario Grade 9 (MPM1D / MTH1W) Expectations Index
publisher: Problem-Based Learning, Enrichment, and Equity Resources
description: Concise, searchable index of every Ontario Grade 9 mathematics expectation with cross-references to topics, sample questions, and worked examples — useful as a quick-lookup teacher reference and for mapping locally produced lessons to MTH1W expectations.

title: Genetics and Probability: Punnett Squares
description: Explore the connection between genetics and probability through Punnett square activities. Students learn how probability determines trait inheritance while practicing fraction and ratio calculations.

title: GeoGebra — IM Grade 7 Math (Equations Lessons Collection)
publisher: Interactive Platforms — Desmos, GeoGebra, PhET, Mathigon
description: Curated GeoGebra Book of applets paired with each IM Grade 7 lesson, including hanger diagrams, balance-equation builders, and inequality number-line tools.

title: GeoGebra — Math 8 Tasks Collection
description: Free curated GeoGebra book of Grade 8 distance-learning activities covering transformations, the Pythagorean theorem, volumes, and angle relationships — with ready-to-assign interactive applets suitable for synchronous or asynchronous instruction.

title: GetSmarterAboutMoney.ca — Ontario Securities Commission
description: Ontario Securities Commission's public financial literacy site. Compound interest calculators, budgeting worksheets, and plain-language articles that Grade 8 teachers can readily adapt for class use or student research.

title: Google Earth - Interactive 3D Mapping Tool
description: Google Earth provides an interactive, photorealistic digital version of our planet, allowing students to explore geographical features, landscapes, and ecosystems from above. Ideal for Earth and Space Systems and STEM skills development through visualization and exploration.

title: Google Teachable Machine — AI/Machine Learning for Grade 7
description: Web tool that makes machine learning accessible to students; train computers to recognize images, sounds, and poses with no coding required; exportable models can be used in Scratch or other platforms for game/app creation.

title: Grade 7 History Bundle (Strands A & B)
description: Complete 47-lesson bundle covering both Grade 7 history strands. Cross-curricular, engaging, and inquiry-based lessons aligned with Ontario 2023 curriculum standards.

title: Grade 7 History Unit 1: New France & British North America, 1713-1800
description: 21 in-depth lessons covering social, political, economic, and legal changes in early Canadian history. Includes content knowledge instruction and investigation activities for deeper learning.

title: Grade 7 Ontario Math Measurement Unit (Complete)
description: Complete Grade 7 E2 Measurement unit with 14 detailed 3-part lessons, activities, assessments, and report card comments covering all Ontario 2020 curriculum expectations.

title: Grade 7 Science STEM Activities (2022 Ontario Curriculum)
description: Comprehensive unit covering Grade 7 STEM Skills and Connections. Includes lesson plans, presentations, worksheets, and hands-on investigations aligned to 2022 Ontario curriculum. Focus on engineering design and scientific research processes.

title: Grade 8 Ontario Measurement Unit (Circumference, Area, Surface Area, Volume)
description: Complete Grade 8 E2 Measurement curriculum covering circumference and area of circles, surface area and volume of prisms and cylinders, aligned to Ontario 2020 curriculum.

title: Historica Canada Education Portal
description: Historica Canada's central education portal with 300+ free learning tools including thematic education guides, classroom-ready Heritage Minutes, and historical thinking activities.

title: Illustrative Mathematics Grade 7 — "Check Your Readiness" & End-of-Unit Assessments
description: Each IM unit begins with a diagnostic "Check Your Readiness" and ends with a unit assessment (longer units also include a mid-unit check). Each item is tagged with the lesson it supports and guidance for responding to student misconceptions.

title: Indigenous Lesson Plans / Teaching Resources — Mathematics (Lakehead University Guide)
description: Lakehead University's library guide to Indigenous lesson plans by subject. Valuable Northern Ontario perspective and connections to local First Nations — particularly useful for teachers serving northern communities.

title: Indigenous Peoples Atlas of Canada
description: Interactive atlas featuring Indigenous perspectives on geography, history, and culture. Created by and for Indigenous peoples with educator resources.

title: Interactivate: Probability Explorations
description: Collection of Java-based probability applets including racing games, experimental vs theoretical comparisons, and Monte Carlo simulations adapted for middle school.

title: Interactive History Simulations (HistorySimulation.com)
description: Interactive decision-making simulations where students experience historical challenges and scenarios. Highly engaging format for understanding cause-and-effect relationships in history.

title: IOM Global Migration Data Portal
publisher: Global Settlement and Migration Data Tools
description: International Organization for Migration's single-window data portal with interactive maps, country profiles, and thematic dashboards (immigration, emigration, public opinion) suitable for student inquiry into migration patterns.

title: IXL — Mathématiques (Curriculum Ontario, version française)
publisher: French Online Practice and Learning Platforms
description: IXL's Ontario math curriculum alignment (some skills available in French via the platform's French interface). Provides thousands of practice skills tagged to Ontario expectations B-F for Grades 7 and 8.

title: IXL — Ontario Grade 7 Math: Financial Literacy Skills Practice
description: IXL's adaptive learning platform with Ontario Grade 7 aligned practice problems on financial literacy. Tracks student progress, provides hints, and adapts difficulty based on performance.

title: IXL — Ontario Grade 7 Science Curriculum
description: IXL's Ontario Grade 7 science skill plan with adaptive practice on ecosystems, energy flow, biotic/abiotic factors, and other Strand B topics. Subscription-based with diagnostic and progress tracking.

title: JA Canada Digital Campus
description: Junior Achievement Canada's free digital campus. Hosts the Grade 8 "Dollars with Sense" program plus dozens of self-directed modules on money management, budgeting, and decision-making — teacher-led or student-paced.

title: Khan Academy — 7th Grade Expressions, Equations, & Inequalities (Unit)
description: Full Khan Academy 7th-grade unit with video lessons, quick-checks, mid-unit and end-of-unit quizzes covering the entire C2 expectation set in a self-paced format.

title: Khan Academy — 8th Grade Math Course Challenge
description: Adaptive course-challenge assessment that samples across the Grade 8 Khan Academy course. Useful as a free end-of-year review or summative-style check, especially for students working independently or in flipped-classroom settings.

title: Khan Academy — Illustrative Math Grade 8, Unit 6: Associations in Data
description: Khan Academy's video and practice support keyed lesson-by-lesson to the Illustrative Mathematics Grade 8 Unit 6 ("Associations in Data") — a strong companion if a class is using IM curriculum.

title: Kids Code Jeunesse / Digital Moment — Digital Literacy Education
description: Canadian nonprofit (now Digital Moment) offering free classroom coding lessons, educator workshops, and CanCode-funded resources designed for bilingual Canadian classrooms.

title: Knowledgehook Gameshow — Free Formative Assessment Tool
description: Free interactive gameshow assessment platform. Teachers can run Ontario-aligned ready-made or custom formatives; the Insights dashboard surfaces misconceptions in real time, making it ideal for Grade 8 minds-on or consolidation checks.

title: L'équipe TacTIC — Centre franco
publisher: Ontario French-Language Boards & Provincial PD Networks
description: TacTIC is the provincial network supporting Ontario's 12 French-language school boards in digital-age teaching — including math-specific PD on technology integration aligned to the 2020 curriculum.

title: Le curriculum de l'Ontario de la 1re à la 8e année — Mathématiques (2020) — Publications Ontario
publisher: Official Ontario Curriculum Documents in French
description: Official Publications Ontario catalogue listing for the 2020 French math curriculum, with metadata (publisher, ISBN, publication date) useful for citation in lesson plans and policy documents.

title: Le curriculum de l'Ontario de la 1re à la 8e année — Mathématiques 2020 (PDF complet)
publisher: Official Ontario Curriculum Documents in French
description: Full downloadable PDF of the 2020 French-language elementary math curriculum. Useful for offline planning and for pulling specific expectation codes when tagging resources.

title: Le curriculum de l'Ontario, de la 1re à la 8e année — Mathématiques (2020)
publisher: Official Ontario Curriculum Documents in French
description: Official French-language curriculum portal from the Ontario Ministry of Education hosting the 2020 mathematics expectations for grades 1–8. Searchable by grade, strand, and overall expectation.

title: Learn.Genetics: Cell Biology and DNA
description: Interactive tutorials on cells, DNA, heredity, and genetics. Includes virtual labs, animations, and printable activities explaining complex biological concepts.

title: LEGO Education MINDSTORMS EV3 Curriculum
description: Comprehensive 30+ hour curriculum with 12+ computer science activities; includes cross-curricular opportunities in design, technology, science, and math with real-world problem-solving.

title: Lifepack Coding — Grade 8 Data Analysis Unit (Ontario)
description: Lifepack Coding's Grade 8 Data Analysis unit — pure Ontario C3 alignment. Includes both plugged (Scratch) and unplugged options, and is structured so teachers can run it with no prior coding experience.

title: Lynx Coding (Canadian, bilingual + Indigenous languages)
description: Canadian-developed coding environment available in English, French, Ojibwe, Mi'kmaw, Oji-Cree, and Mohawk. Ideal scaffold for Grade 7 students moving from blocks toward text; supports sub-programs and nested control structures.

title: Make Math Moments — MTH1W Grade 9 De-streamed Math Teacher Supports
description: Kyle Pearce and Jon Orr's MTH1W hub with 3-act tasks, problem-based units, and planning supports emphasizing the math-fluency-and-modelling aspect of the de-streamed course.

title: Maneuvering the Middle – Probability Unit (7th Grade)
description: A structured 9-day probability unit covering simple probability, experimental and theoretical probability, making predictions, and independent/dependent events with real-world application questions and error analysis tasks.

title: Maneuvering the Middle — Free Math Performance Tasks (Middle School)
description: Curated free task collection from Maneuvering the Middle. Tasks come with student handouts and rubrics; multiple tasks map directly onto Grade 8 Ontario E2 (volume), C1/C2 (linear relations), and D1 (statistical reasoning) expectations.

title: MapleMath — Ontario Grade 7 Number Strand
description: Ontario-focused adaptive math platform featuring Canadian-context narrative lessons (e.g. Kensington Market scenarios) with 350+ interactive lessons, adaptive question banks, and CCAT reasoning practice.

title: MARS / Mathematics Assessment Project — Classroom Challenges
description: Developed by the University of Nottingham Shell Centre, these research-based formative assessment lessons expose student thinking and include both short diagnostic tasks and complex "Expert Task" problems, with detailed rubrics and teacher moves for each lesson.

title: Math Catcher: Mathematics Through Aboriginal Storytelling (Simon Fraser University)
description: Dr. Veselin Jungic's program producing bilingual picture books and animated math stories in Blackfoot, Cree, Squamish, Nisga'a, and more. Stories embed math problems that Grade 7–8 teachers can scale up as extension tasks.

title: Math First Peoples Teacher Resource Guide (FNESC, 2020)
publisher: Teaching Units, Lesson Plans, and Practitioner Guides
description: FNESC's updated (2020) teacher resource guide for Grades 8–9 mathematics, developed with First Nations educators. Incorporates interconnectedness, place-based learning, storytelling, and specific unit plans that map cleanly onto Ontario Grade 8 expectations.

title: Math is Fun: Data and Graphs
description: Clear explanations of data concepts with interactive examples. Covers types of graphs, measures of central tendency, and data interpretation with practice problems.

title: Mathador.fr — Calcul mental ludique
publisher: Mental Math, Calcul Mental & Game-based Practice
description: Mental-math game platform (Chrono, Solo, weekly Concours) with a teacher dashboard tracking student progress; widely used in 5e and 4e (≈ Ontario Grades 7–8) for fluency-building.

title: Mathies Data Tools
description: Interactive tools from the Ontario Ministry of Education for exploring data visualization, including bar graphs, pictographs, and circle graphs. Students can input their own data and see it represented in multiple formats.

title: Mathies Learning Tools (Ontario Ministry-developed)
description: Ontario Ministry of Education's official suite of virtual manipulatives (algebra tiles, fraction strips, number charts, set tools) available as HTML5 web tools and mobile apps.

title: Mathigon — The Mathematical Playground
description: Mathigon's innovative interactive courses cover fractions, prime numbers, exponents, and rational numbers with step-by-step storytelling, virtual manipulatives, and guided exploration suitable for Grade 8 Number Sense.

title: Mathspace – Ontario Grade 7 & 8 Data Topics
description: Mathspace's Ontario 2021 Edition textbook chapters on data, with worked examples, interactive applets, and instructional videos. Covers categorical vs. numerical data, graph construction, and critical interpretation of statistics in media.

title: Mathspace — Grade 7 & 8 Geometry & Measurement
description: Australian-based adaptive platform with strong support for students. Includes step-by-step solutions, automatic marking, and immediate feedback. Strong focus on conceptual understanding alongside procedural fluency.

title: Mathspace — Ontario Grade 7 & 8 Measurement (Interactive)
description: Interactive Ontario-aligned platform with worked examples, applets, and adaptive practice for measurement concepts across Grade 7-8.

title: Mathspace — Ontario Grade 7 2021 Edition
description: Curriculum-aligned Ontario Grade 7 math textbook with video lessons, worked examples, and adaptive practice for writing rules for relationships and solving contextual algebra problems.

title: mBlock — Robotics and STEM Coding Platform
description: Powerful coding platform based on Scratch 3.0, specifically designed for STEM and robotics learning. Supports progression from block-based to Python coding. Can program Makeblock robots and open-source hardware.

title: Mi'kmawey Debert Cultural Centre — Teaching About the Mi'kmaq (School Curriculum)
description: Mi'kmawey Debert's authoritative, community-developed curriculum collection. Lessons on the 13-moon calendar (calendar mathematics), beadwork patterns, and traditional games of chance (e.g., Waltes/Hubbub) extend cleanly into Ontario 7–8.

title: Minecraft Education Edition — Geometry Curriculum
description: Minecraft's curriculum lessons for geometry include building 3D shapes, understanding coordinates, and spatial problem-solving.

title: Mining Matters — Classroom Resource Kits (PDAC)
description: Set of 39 classroom-ready earth-science and mining lesson plans with student activity cards, posters, and resource samples; aligned to provincial geography and earth-science curricula.

title: MIT App Inventor — Mobile Application Development
description: MIT's free platform for creating Android and iOS mobile applications; teaches computational thinking through real-world app development; students drag-and-drop components and use visual blocks for logic.

title: Mon Classeur de Mathématiques — Cycle 4 (5e, 4e, 3e)
description: France-based teacher site with organized lessons, interactive exercises, and video walkthroughs for Cycle 4 (ages 12–15). Useful as a parallel resource for content alignment and for students seeking varied explanations in French.

title: Money and Youth — CFEE Guide to Financial Literacy
description: Canadian Foundation for Economic Education's flagship youth financial literacy resource — over 740,000 copies in Canadian schools. Values-based approach covering spending, saving, borrowing, and protecting money. Age-appropriate for Grade 8.

title: Money Mentors — Grade 7 Financial Literacy Activities & Worksheets
description: Canadian non-profit's free educational materials including "Banking on Your Future" series introducing bank accounts and services, plus worksheets on spending plans and net worth calculations.

title: Money School Canada Workshops - Financial Literacy
description: Money School Canada will do online or in-classroom workshops on the topic of Financial Literacy.

title: My Math Path 8 — Ontario Curriculum Correlation Document
description: Detailed correlation chart showing exactly which Grade 8 expectations each lesson and assessment in My Math Path 8 covers. Useful even for teachers not using the program — they can adopt the mapping as a self-assessment blueprint.

title: My Math Path 8 — Ontario Edition (Nelson)
description: Trillium-Listed Grade 1–8 program. The Grade 8 edition includes Progress Checks (EQAO-style formatives) and full unit summatives mapped to specific 2020 Ontario expectations; OECM-approved as a Math Skills Digital Tool.

title: NASA Climate Kids — Big Questions

description: Official Ontario 2020 curriculum expectations for Grade 8 Number Sense, including rational and irrational numbers, exponential notation, and understanding the real number system.

description: Authoritative Ontario Ministry of Education page listing all Grade 8 Strand B specific expectations for the Cells unit. Foundational reference for any Grade 8 teacher planning a Life Systems unit.

description: Official Ontario Ministry page listing all Grade 8 Strand D expectations covering systems, mechanical advantage, work, efficiency, and automation. Authoritative source for planning.

description: Official strand-by-strand overview that situates Strand B (Cells) within the full Grade 8 Science program, useful for long-range planning that integrates Strand A skills into the cells unit.

title: Ontario Curriculum: Scientific and Engineering Design Processes (Strand A)
description: Ministry resource explaining the engineering design process embedded in Strand A across all grades. Critical for unpacking design challenges in Strand D and beyond.

description: Official Ontario digital curriculum portal with searchable Grade 8 history expectations, sample inquiry questions, and links to the 2023-revised curriculum that replaces the 2018 document.

description: Comprehensive Ontario curriculum resource for all Spatial Sense components, including classroom activities and assessment strategies.

description: Official Ministry alignment chart showing how Grade 7 and 8 Number expectations build toward Grade 9 MTH1W expectations, helping teachers scaffold Number strand learning across grade bands.

title: Ontario Mathematics Curriculum – Data Strand Continuum (Grades 1–8)
description: Official Ontario curriculum expectations continuum for the Data strand, showing how data literacy skills develop from Grade 1 through Grade 8. Useful for understanding vertical alignment and prerequisite skills.

title: Ontario Mathematics Curriculum, Grade 7 — Strand D2: Probability
description: Authoritative source for Grade 7 D2 expectations from Ontario's Mathematics Curriculum and Resources portal. Contains the overall and specific expectations, teacher prompts, and "examples" for independent/dependent events.

title: Ontario Ministry Mathematics Curriculum homepage
description: Entry point to the full curriculum with built-in search and filter tools; useful for pulling specific-expectation text into lesson-plan headers.

title: Ontario Ministry of Education — Grade 7 Math Curriculum, Strand C (Algebra)
description: Authoritative source for every Grade 7 Strand C expectation (including all of C3 Coding), with instructional examples, teacher prompts, and example student work embedded in the curriculum portal.

title: Ontario Number Strand Curriculum Document (AODA PDF)
publisher: Official Ontario Curriculum Documents and Portal
description: Accessible PDF of the Ontario 2020 Mathematics Curriculum for the Number strand, showing Grade 7 expectations in context with the full K–8 progression so teachers can see prior learning and next steps.

title: Ontario Teachers' Federation — Grade 7/8 History Resources
description: Practical accommodated and modified lesson units designed for ESL and diverse learners. Includes simplified texts, accessibility features, and structured lesson plans aligned with Ontario curriculum.

title: Ontario Teachers' Federation — Teaching Financial Literacy (OTF Connects)
description: OTF Connects on-demand session for Ontario teachers on delivering Grade 7-8 financial literacy, including currency conversion, simple/compound interest, and goal-setting strategies.

title: Ontario.ca — Grade 1–8 Mathematics Curriculum Overview
description: Top-level portal for the 2020 Ontario elementary mathematics curriculum. Useful starting point for parents/teachers wanting to situate Grade 8 D1 within the larger curriculum vision.

title: Open Middle — Creating Inequalities (and related algebra tasks)
description: Open Middle problem by Robert Kaplinsky asking students to use integers −4 to 4 at most once to create an inequality with solution x > 2/3 — exemplifies the open-middle format for rich C2 reasoning.

title: Open Middle — Cylinders
description: Open Middle cylinder task asking students to find integer dimensions for a cylindrical tank that holds a volume in a given range (e.g., 110–115 cubic feet). Reinforces volume formula reasoning with constraint-based problem-solving.

title: Open Middle — Line Builders
description: Open Middle problem where students fill in tables to create different linear relations meeting given constraints. Encourages flexible thinking about how patterns generate different equations.

title: Open Middle — Maximizing Rectangular Prism Volume
description: An Open Middle (Robert Kaplinsky / Nanette Johnson) problem in which students must place digits 1–9 to create the rectangular prism with the greatest possible volume. Lightweight, low-floor / high-ceiling task.

title: Open Middle — Maximizing Rectangular Prism Volume Versus Surface Area
description: A more advanced Open Middle problem connecting Grade 8 surface area and volume work to optimization, an early algebra/measurement bridge.

title: Open Middle — Pythagorean Theorem
description: Open Middle task asking students to use digits 0–9 (each at most once) to place values in boxes and find pairs of possible side lengths in a right triangle. Encourages multiple solution paths and deeper reasoning.

title: Open Middle — Rational and Irrational Numbers
description: Open Middle's challenging problems on rational and irrational numbers — students fill in boxes using digits 1–8 to create numbers meeting specific constraints; promotes depth of understanding through multiple solution paths.

title: Open Middle — Rectangular Prism Surface Area Versus Volume
description: An Open Middle task that asks students to find prism dimensions where surface area and volume are equal. Excellent for surfacing the conceptual distinction between the two measures.

title: Open Middle — Solving Equations with Variables on Both Sides
description: Students place digits 1-9 to satisfy a constraint, with no/infinite/unique solution variants. Encourages reasoning, structure-spotting, and verification rather than rote symbol manipulation. Pairs well with IM Unit 4 lesson 7.

title: Open Middle — Two-Step Equations
publisher: Enrichment & Open-Ended Tasks — Open Middle, NRICH
description: Open Middle DOK 3 task in which Grade 7 students use the digits 1–9 at most once each to create a two-step equation that yields a specific value of x, sparking creative reasoning about equation structure.

title: Polypad by Amplify – Probability & Data Tools
description: Free, no-login digital math playground with drag-and-drop data tables, statistical plots, and charting tools. Supports direct integration into Desmos Activity Builder for classroom use.

title: Polypad by Amplify — Virtual Manipulatives for Geometry
description: Interactive digital manipulatives for exploring geometry concepts including tessellations with regular polygons, symmetry using virtual mirrors, and measurement with protractors, with assignable activities for teachers.

title: Practical Money Skills Canada
description: Free financial education curriculum with lesson plans, games, and calculators. Topics include budgeting basics, saving strategies, and understanding credit???all designed for Canadian students.

title: Probability Station Activity - Theoretical vs. Experimental
description: This interactive probability game has students explore and compare theoretical and experimental probabilities.

title: Prodigy Math — Grade 8 Math Curriculum
description: Canadian-made RPG-style math game with 1,500+ curriculum-aligned skills covering Grade 8 Number topics including integers, rational numbers, exponents, and operations; free for teachers with optional premium home tier.

title: Prodigy Math: Probability Adventures
description: Game-based learning platform with adaptive probability questions embedded in an RPG adventure. Students practice probability concepts while earning rewards and progressing through storylines.

title: Projet de Ressources Curriculaires Mathématiques (OAME et AFEMO) — Édusource
description: OAME/AFEMO joint project funded to support 2020 curriculum implementation. Includes course-plan models organized by concept grouping, plus French-language professional learning webinars.

title: Queen's Engineering — Grade 7 Form and Function Unit Plan
description: Queen's University Faculty of Engineering's complete Grade 7 unit plan on Form and Function, organized as a multi-lesson engineering design sequence aligned to Ontario expectations.

title: Queen's University Library — Coding in Primary/Junior Math Guide
description: Academic library guide aggregating vetted coding resources for Ontario primary/junior mathematics teachers; includes recommendations, evaluation criteria, and links to platforms and lesson collections.

title: Queen's University Library — Grade 7 Geography Research Guide
description: Curated academic library guide collating government, NGO, and educator-vetted resources for Ontario Grade 7 Geography teachers — useful as a meta-resource for educator PD.

title: Raspberry Pi Foundation — Learn to Code
description: Non-profit Raspberry Pi Foundation library with structured Grade 7-appropriate coding projects, including a screen-turtle geometry unit directly applicable to C3 classroom tasks.

title: Rethinking Mathematics: Teaching Social Justice by the Numbers (Rethinking Schools)

title: RÉCIT MST — Ressources pédagogiques
publisher: Quebec Francophone Platforms (used cross-border in Ontario classrooms)

title: Scholastic Teachables — Measurement & Geometry Lesson Plans & Worksheets
description: Scholastic's curated collection of measurement lesson plans and practice worksheets aligned to U.S./Canadian standards. Clear visual supports and multiple difficulty levels.

title: Science North — Downloadable Educator Resources
description: Free downloadable resources from Science North, including handouts on what makes structures strong and stable, and elements that lend support and balance.

title: Science North — Grade 7 Coding and Math Lessons
description: Free Grade 7 lessons including algorithms-and-geometry activities using Ozobots, Tinkercad + Codeblocks structure design, and Cue robot coding — cross-curricular math/science coding tasks with Ontario alignment.

title: Science Teachers' Association of Ontario (STAO) — Coding Lesson Plans by Grade
description: STAO's bank of coding lesson plans organized by grade level, widely referenced in Ontario teacher-education library guides as a go-to source for Grade 7 classroom-ready coding activities.

title: Scientists in School — Strong Structures Teacher Resource Package
description: Canadian charity Scientists in School's free teacher resource package on strong structures, including background information and hands-on activities suitable for classroom delivery.

title: Shodor Interactivate – Histogram & Box Plot Activities

title: STAO — Grade 9 Earth and Space Science Lesson Plans
description: Curated library of searchable lesson plans and classroom resources aligned to Ontario Grade 9 Earth and Space Science curriculum, including hands-on activities and demonstrations.

title: Sésamath — Manuel cycle 4 (PDF complet)

title: TD Bank's Financial Literacy for Kids Website
description: TD Bank provides suggested activities for parents and teachers related to financial literacy. All activities are sorted by age group.

title: TeachEngineering — Bridges Curricular Unit
description: Five-lesson NSF-funded TeachEngineering unit on bridges covering arch, beam, truss, and suspension types with hands-on activities. Excellent for Grade 7 Form and Function.

description: Full Grade 8 Strand E workbook covering scale factor, tessellations, measurement units, triangles/quadrilaterals, circles, the Pythagorean theorem, and perimeter/area/volume/circumference. Includes a mini-golf design project, tessellation activity, quiz, and unit test aligned to Ontario 2020 expectations.

title: Teachers Pay Teachers — Grade 7 Ontario Math Full Year Bundle
description: Comprehensive year-long bundle with 1000+ pages of aligned materials. Includes specific measurement units with visual supports, differentiated tasks, and assessment tools.

title: Techthings.ca — Coding Program Inventory (Ontario curriculum integration)
description: Teacher-curated comparison of coding platforms (Scratch, Lynx, micro:bit, Ozobot, etc.) mapped to Ontario curriculum. Helps a Grade 7 teacher quickly select the right tool for a specific C3.1/C3.2 sub-expectation.

title: The Ontario Curriculum Grades 1–8 (2023 Official Document)
description: Complete 2023 Ontario curriculum document in accessible PDF format. Contains all curriculum expectations, learning contexts, equity considerations, and assessment guidance.

description: Focused E1 unit covering tessellations, scale drawings, and perspective views for Grade 8, aligned to the Ontario 2020 curriculum with lessons, worksheets, review activities, and tests.

description: Transum's transformations topic page collects 30+ self-marking interactive exercises and lesson starters across 7 difficulty levels. Students reflect shapes across mirror lines by clicking grid coordinates — immediate feedback and teacher-track answers make this an efficient practice tool.

title: Treaty Teaching and Learning Resources — Government of Ontario
description: Provincially curated collection of treaty education materials including Treaty Recognition Week activities, maps, and primary documents directly aligned to the Indigenous content in Grade 8 Strand A.

title: Turtle Diary: Probability Games for Kids

title: TVO Digital Learning Outreach – Coding in the Classroom: Intermediate Mathematics

title: TVO Outreach — Coding Transformations (cross-grade lesson)

title: TVO Webinars – Math & Financial Literacy (Grades 7–8)
description: Recorded TVO webinars designed for Ontario educators teaching financial literacy in the intermediate grades, with practical strategies and classroom examples.

title: U of T Engineering Outreach — Educator Resources Database
description: University of Toronto Engineering Outreach's curated database of free online resources mapped directly to Ontario science curriculum expectations, with strong representation in structures and engineering design.

description: A free four-lesson curriculum supplement teaching Grade 7–9 math concepts through personal finance examples, including "The Secret to Becoming a Millionaire" (compound interest), budget spreading, and math in taxes.

description: Upper Canada District School Board library guide that organizes vetted resources by Strand B sub-topic; bilingual, includes NFB educational playlists with study guides.

publisher: Global data and visualization tools (HDI / GDP / SDGs)

title: Unit 4: Proportional Relationships and Percentages
description: Complete unit combining proportional reasoning with percentage applications, allowing students to apply ratio and proportion concepts to real-world percentage problems.

title: Unit 4: Proportional Relationships with Tables
description: Introduces proportional relationships by examining tables of values, helping students identify the constant rate of change and distinguish proportional from non-proportional relationships.

title: Unit 8: Lesson 1 - Intro to Probability

title: United for Literacy — Grade 8 Financial Literacy Resource Guide (PDF)
description: United for Literacy's Grade 8 financial literacy teaching guide with lesson structures, "Teacher Talk Moves" discussion supports, and real-world scenarios aligned to Grade 8 F1.

title: Upper Canada Virtual Library — Earth & Space Grade 9 LibGuide
description: Comprehensive LibGuide for Grade 9 Applied and Academic Science students in Ontario, featuring information, lesson plans, and curated resource lists for Earth and Space topics.

title: Virtual Bead Loom (Culturally Situated Design Tools — Grade 8 version)
description: Specific CSDT tool where students replicate Indigenous basket and beadwork designs using coordinate geometry on a four-quadrant plane. Directly maps to Grade 8 Spatial Sense expectations on the Cartesian plane.
