title: Virtual Museum of New France (Canadian Museum of History)
publisher: Government Archives and Primary Source Collections
description: Canadian Museum of History's bilingual virtual exhibit covering New France from 1534-1763 with interactive maps, artifact images, and short documentary videos suitable for inquiry.

title: What Is Matter? Definition and Examples
description: Comprehensive educational resource defining matter in science with practical examples and explanations of different forms. Suitable for students learning foundational concepts about physical substances and their properties.

title: Wooclap: Real-Time Data Collection
description: Interactive polling tool that lets teachers collect real-time data from students. Perfect for demonstrating data collection, visualization, and analysis with authentic class-generated data.

title: Wordwall — Interactive Games & Quizzes (Area, Perimeter, Volume)
description: Crowdsourced library of 100+ interactive Wordwall games for measurement practice. Teachers and students create and share games; formats include match-ups, quizzes, group sorts, and racing games.

title: Youcubed – Probability and Data Tasks
description: Stanford's Youcubed offers probability-related tasks designed to develop mathematical mindsets. Activities emphasize visual and creative approaches to probability with a focus on depth over speed.

title: Youcubed — Mathematical Mindset Algebra
description: Stanford Youcubed's algebra unit that introduces variables through pattern growth before "solving for x" — directly compatible with the Ontario C1 philosophy of linear pattern-based algebra in Grade 8.

title: YouCubed — Visual Math Activities by Jo Boaler
description: YouCubed resources from Stanford emphasize visual and physical representations in geometry learning, providing open-ended tasks and video cases of effective geometry teaching that builds geometric intuition.

title: 5 Fun Financial Literacy Activities For Kids
description: 5 Fun Financial Literacy Activities For Kids is hosted on the Rates.ca website and provides ideas for activities related to financial literacy for parents and teachers

title: 8 Ways — Aboriginal Pedagogy: Maths
description: Australia-originated framework that Ontario teachers reference for structuring math lessons around Indigenous learning processes; includes math-specific examples for intermediate grades.

title: Aboriginal Math and Science — Elementary Lesson Plans (Queen's Aboriginal Access to Engineering)
publisher: Teaching Units, Lesson Plans, and Practitioner Guides
description: Queen's University's curated bank of Indigenous math and science lesson plans, targeting Grades 4–8. Includes Indigenous Perspectives in Math units and other Ontario-relevant resources.

title: ATB Financial: Money Management for Youth
description: Alberta-focused financial literacy resources including budgeting tools, saving calculators, and lessons on making smart financial decisions as a young person.

title: Atlas of Canada — Climate and Environment Maps
description: NRCan's curated set of climate and environment maps showing Canadian temperature, precipitation, ecozones, and vegetation patterns — a primary-source companion to climate-change inquiry.

title: Atlas of Canada — Historical Atlas Online (NRCan)
description: Natural Resources Canada's official Atlas of Canada with historical and contemporary map layers students can use to investigate New France settlement patterns and the geographic spread of the fur trade.

title: Atlas of Canada — Minerals and Mining
description: Specialized Atlas of Canada interactive layer showing mineral deposits, mines, and processing facilities, useful for case-study mapping in resource-extraction inquiries.

title: Bank of Canada Museum — Learning Resources by Grade
description: Filterable hub that groups Bank of Canada Museum activities, videos, lesson plans, and games by grade level so teachers can quickly find Grade 7-aligned financial literacy material.

title: Bank of Canada — Financial Education Resources
description: Official Bank of Canada educational resources including interactive tools, videos, and lesson materials supporting understanding of Canadian money and financial concepts.

title: ByteLearn — 7th Grade Expressions, Equations, and Inequalities Worksheets
description: Each ByteLearn worksheet has a free printable PDF and an auto-graded interactive twin students can complete online, generating instant teacher reports — useful for blended classrooms.

title: Canadian Encyclopedia: Indigenous Peoples
description: Comprehensive articles on First Nations, M??tis, and Inuit peoples, cultures, histories, and contemporary issues. Includes educator resources and study guides.

title: Canadian Geographic Education — Indigenous Peoples Atlas of Canada (Tiled Map)
description: Free downloadable tiled version of the Indigenous Peoples Atlas of Canada giant floor map, focused on historical treaties, language families, and Indigenous nations whose territories framed New France and early BNA.

title: Canadian Geographic Education — Natural Resources Canada Giant Floor Map
description: Bilingual giant floor map produced with NRCan featuring Canada's forests, freshwater, oil and gas, minerals, and mining; bundled with curriculum-aligned lesson plans for intermediate grades.

title: Canadian Museum of Immigration: Pier 21
description: Resources on Canadian immigration history focusing on Pier 21 in Halifax. Includes oral histories, primary documents, and interactive timelines.

title: CAVLFO — Cours en ligne de mathématiques (7e/8e année)
publisher: Ontario French-Language Government and Ministry-Backed Platforms
description: Consortium d'apprentissage virtuel de langue française de l'Ontario — provides accredited online math courses for Ontario francophone students, covering full curriculum expectations.

title: Certain, likely, equally likely, unlikely or impossible
description: This video talks about probability - certain, likely, equally likely, unlikely or impossible events.

title: CIRA: Data Privacy for Students
description: Lessons on data privacy, online tracking, and digital footprints. Students learn how their data is collected and used while developing critical thinking about data in the digital age.

title: CK-12 — Block and Tackle Simulation
description: CK-12 interactive simulation exploring the relationship between force, work, energy, and mechanical advantage in pulley systems. Highly relevant to Grade 8 Systems in Action.

title: CK-12 — See Saw Simulation (Torque & Mechanical Advantage)
description: CK-12 simulation showing how objects of different weights balance on a lever. Useful concrete demonstration of mechanical advantage for both grades.

title: Climate Data Viewer — Canada.ca
description: Government of Canada climate data viewer with maps of temperature, precipitation, snow, and projected change; includes downloadable datasets for primary-source classroom work.

title: Code and build a device to collect temperature data
description: Simple Data Statistics (Temperature) - Arduino collects and calculates basic statistics on the acquired analog (temperature) data: average, standard deviation, min and max.

title: CodeHS – Coding in Math
description: JavaScript and Python-based course integrating coding with math concepts including area/perimeter calculations, geometric shape drawing, and categorizing triangles using variables.

title: Concord Consortium — Molecular Workbench: Mixtures and Solutions
description: Concord Consortium's open-licence physical science library hosts molecular-scale interactive models students can manipulate to explore why some substances mix and others don't. Strong fit for inquiry-based Grade 7 Strand C investigations.

title: Credit Canada: Youth Financial Wellness
description: Non-profit resources teaching credit basics, the dangers of debt, and responsible borrowing. Includes interactive calculators showing how interest accumulates over time.

title: Crown-Indigenous Relations — Specific Claim Settlements Interactive Map (SCSIM)
publisher: Government Archives and Primary Source Records
description: Government of Canada interactive map showing settled and active Indigenous specific claims across Canada, supporting student inquiry into the geographic and sustainability implications of land use and Indigenous land rights.

title: CSViamonde — TFO IDÉLLO Apprendre à la maison
publisher: Ontario French-Language Boards & Provincial PD Networks
description: Conseil scolaire Viamonde's curated entry point into TFO IDÉLLO's home-learning portal, with quick links to grade-level math activities for Grades 7 and 8.

title: D2L Adventure — De-streamed Grade 9 Ontario Math
description: D2L's gamified, story-driven MTH1W course mapped to the 2021 curriculum, designed for use inside Brightspace with built-in formative assessments and adaptive pathways.

title: Desmos Activities for Middle School Geometry & Measurement
description: Desmos offers free classroom activities where students explore measurement concepts (especially circles and area relationships) through interactive graphing and visual feedback. Teacher dashboard provides real-time student data.

title: Desmos Geometry Tool
description: Desmos Geometry allows students to plot and connect points to create polygons, measure angles, and verify geometric relationships. Dynamic environment supports discovery activities such as deriving the sum of interior angles of any polygon using the formula (n-2)180.

title: Desmos – What Are Probabilities? (Grades 7–8 Lesson 3)
description: An interactive Desmos activity where students explore the meaning of probability, place events on a likelihood scale, and develop understanding of probability as a number between 0 and 1.

title: Desmos — Calculatrice graphique (FR)
publisher: Interactive Math Platforms with Full French Interface
description: Free graphing calculator with full French-language interface. Supports Grade 7–8 work on linear relations and the Grade 8 introduction to scatter plots, plus extension to MTH1W.

title: Drawing Data with Kids: A Screen-Free Journey through the Art of Visualization for Kids
description: In this interactive book, children aged 7-14 will embark on a screen-free journey, discovering the magic of data through hands-on drawing exercises.

title: EconEdLink — Making a Budget: It Is All Spending!
description: Council for Economic Education lesson that walks students through identifying income vs. spending categories and constructing a working budget — strong fit for F1.4.

title: EconEdLink — The Basics of Saving and Budgeting
description: Personal-finance lesson on identifying ways to spend and save in order to reach financial goals; pairs naturally with F1.3 and provides extension scenarios for differentiation.

title: Edugain Canada — Grade 8 Math Practice and Worksheets
description: Canadian-facing practice platform with free printable and online Grade 8 algebra worksheets covering expressions, equations, inequalities, and pattern rules — useful for formative practice and homework.

title: EduGains — TIPS4Math Grade 7 (Three-Part Lesson Plans)
publisher: Official Curriculum Documents and Ministry Supports
description: Ministry-developed three-part lesson plans organized by Grade 7 expectations. The measurement units include diagnostic, action, and consolidation tasks aligned to the Ontario curriculum's E2 expectations.

title: Edutopia – Coding a BBC Micro:bit to Explore Math Problems in Middle School
description: Practical classroom guide describing how teachers use micro:bit for probability simulations, exponential decay experiments, and random number generators in math class.

title: Edwin App — Ontario Math
description: Ontario-specific app with ready-to-use interactive lessons, digital practice, and coding integration for Grade 7-8 algebra, patterns, and computational thinking.

title: Edwin — Financial Literacy: My Dream Vacation
description: Edwin (Nelson) project where Grade 7 students plan a "dream vacation," applying decimal operations, payment methods, and budgeting to make and justify financial choices.

title: Edwin — Social Studies (Nelson)
description: Nelson's curriculum-aligned digital platform with searchable, customizable Social Studies pathways, including Indigenous-authored content, primary sources, and inquiry tools for Strand A.

title: En avant, les maths! — 7e année Concepts mathématiques
publisher: Ontario Government-Funded Teacher Platforms in French
description: Direct entry point to the Grade 7 concept pages. Each topic links to mini-lessons, exemplars, and consolidation tasks aligned to specific overall and specific expectations.

title: En avant, les maths! — 8e année Mini-leçons
publisher: Ontario Government-Funded Teacher Platforms in French
description: Curated short problems for the Grade 8 classroom, designed to be used in small-group or whole-class guided discovery. Each lesson includes a follow-up problem for student verification of understanding.

title: EQAO Grade 9 Assessment of Mathematics
description: EQAO's online Grade 9 math assessment (MTH1W) administered Spring 2026 between May 28 and June 24. Released questions, formula sheet, and the 2025–2026 user guide are all public, making this a free source of high-quality item banks for Grade 7–8 review.

title: EQAO – Mathematics Released Questions by Strand (Grade 6, Junior Division)
description: Official EQAO repository of released Grade 6 junior math questions organized by strand, including Data Management items with sample student responses, rubrics, and knowledge/skills category mapping — valuable for Grade 7–8 formative/diagnostic assessment.

title: Esri Canada — Mapping Wildlife and Natural Resources with ArcGIS
description: Esri Canada lesson hub with ArcGIS activities and StoryMaps on Canadian wildlife and natural resources, including Atlantic fisheries case studies and human-impact analysis.

title: Facing History: Human Rights Education
description: Resources on genocide, human rights, and civic responsibility. Case studies include the Holocaust, residential schools, and contemporary human rights issues.

title: Financial Literacy for Kids
description: This video discusses whether something is a necessity or we just want that thing. The points are easy to follow and understand. By the end, students will receive a good foundation of these points of financial literacy.

title: Fisheries and Oceans Canada — Stewardship and Sustainability Lesson Plans
description: DFO library of K-12 lesson plans on aquatic ecosystems, fisheries stewardship, and sustainability — relevant for natural-resource case studies on water and fish stocks.

title: Fishtank Plus — Expanded Assessment Package (Grade 8)
description: Three-tiered assessment package (pre, mid, post) for every Grade 8 unit. Each assessment ships with a teacher analysis guide that diagnoses likely misconceptions and points to specific lessons for re-teaching — directly usable as a Grade 8 assessment-for-learning system.

title: Gapminder: Dollar Street
description: Explore how families at different income levels live around the world through photos and data. Students analyze economic data while developing global awareness and data interpretation skills.

title: GeoGebra Elementary Math Applets (Sec 1–4)
description: Curated collection of 50+ pre-built GeoGebra applets specifically for grade 7–8 measurement and geometry, including tools for angles, area, perimeter, and volume.

title: GeoGebra – Probability Calculator and Simulations
description: GeoGebra's probability tools allow students to build and run simulations using the RandomBetween function, visualize probability distributions, and explore compound events through dynamic applets.

title: GeoGebra — Area, Surface Area, Volume — Nets
description: A curated GeoGebra collection of net-folding animations for cubes, prisms, and pyramids that helps students see the connection between the 2D net (area) and the 3D solid (surface area, volume).

title: GeoGebra — Dice Roll Simulation
description: Simulate rolling 1, 2, or 3 standard dice and explore the distribution of sums. Strong for introducing sample spaces and the difference between equally-likely outcomes (one die) and non-uniform outcomes (sum of two dice).

title: GeoGebra — Discover Pythagorean Theorem
description: Guided discovery GeoGebra applet where students explore the relationship among squares built on the sides of a right triangle to derive the Pythagorean theorem visually before formalizing it algebraically.

title: Geometric Transformations
description: Students explore and practice geometric transformations including translations, reflections, rotations, and dilations. The activity develops understanding of how transformations affect geometric figures.

title: Google Earth Education — Voyager and Creation Tools
description: Free, web-based virtual globe with lesson plans and the Voyager guided-tour feature — students can build their own projects mapping landforms and physical patterns.

title: Google Sheets Data Analysis Activities
description: Comprehensive guide to teaching students data analysis with Google Sheets including sorting, filtering, formulas, creating charts and graphs, and conditional formatting.

title: Historica Canada — Indigenous Perspectives Education Guide
description: 16-activity guide that brings Indigenous worldviews on land and natural resources into intermediate classrooms; aligned with Canadian curricula and developed with First Nations, Inuit, and Métis partners.

title: Indigenous Peoples Atlas of Canada — Giant Floor Map & Teacher's Guide (Canadian Geographic)
description: Canadian Geographic's loanable 11m × 8m Indigenous Peoples floor map, plus a 60+ page teacher's guide with scale, distance, and proportional-reasoning activities. Free to book for Ontario classrooms; exceptional Grade 7–8 measurement and data unit anchor.

title: Indigenous Teacher Resources — Mathematics (Ontario Tech University Guide)
description: Ontario Tech University's subject-specific research guide for Indigenous K–12 math resources, curated by Ontario librarians. Complementary to the U of T guide with different resource selection.

title: Infusing Indigenous Perspectives and Indigenous Ways of Knowing into Mathematics (Ontario Teachers' Federation)
description: OTF report documenting Ontario teachers' collaborative work on Indigenous perspectives in math, including wigwam geometry, beadwork patterning, and quill box construction. Ontario-centred and grounded in classroom practice.

title: Introduction to Parallel Lines
description: Students build an algebraic understanding that parallel lines have the same slope and may have different y-intercepts. Interactive exploration helps students make connections between geometric and algebraic representations.

title: Investor Education: Stock Market Basics
description: Introduction to investing concepts for young learners, including how the stock market works, the difference between saving and investing, and the power of compound interest over time.

title: JUMP Math — Assessment & Practice Book 7.1 (Canadian Edition)
description: Part 1 of JUMP Math's Grade 7 Canadian-edition Assessment & Practice book, with built-in formative checks and term-end review. Aligned to Ontario expectations when used with the ON support package.

title: JUMP Math — Assessment & Practice Book 7.2 (Canadian Edition)
description: Companion Part 2 workbook completing JUMP Math's Grade 7 assessment-and-practice sequence. Together with 7.1, gives full curriculum coverage with embedded short-cycle assessments.

title: JUMP Math — Coding Lessons (Grades 1–8)
description: JUMP Math Canada's free coding lesson library for Ontario teachers, released to support the 2020 curriculum's coding strand. Filterable by grade so Grade 8 teachers can quickly find C3-aligned starting points.

title: Junior Achievement: Economics for Success
description: In-class volunteer-led program teaching students about personal finance, career exploration, and the connection between education and future earning potential. Sessions cover budgeting, saving, and financial goal-setting.

title: Kahoot! Schools — Math
description: Widely-used game-based assessment tool. Ideal for quick review, vocabulary front-loading, and mid-chapter checks. Premade Grade 7–8 math kahoots are available; teachers can adapt or create their own for Ontario strand coverage.

title: Kahoot! — Mixtures and Solutions
description: Kahoot's searchable bank of Grade 7 mixtures and solutions quizzes. Quick game-based review tool that pairs naturally with the Crash Course Kids video clips.

title: Khan Academy - 3Blue1Brown Collaboration
description: Collaborative videos between Khan Academy and 3Blue1Brown creator Grant Sanderson showing visual approaches to mathematical concepts.

title: Khan Academy — One-Step Inequalities Practice
description: Targeted practice set on one-step inequalities with adaptive feedback, hints, and worked-example videos for each missed problem.

title: Khan Academy — Two-Step Inequalities Practice
description: Practice set focused on two-step inequalities, paired with the corresponding skill video so teachers can assign as a station or homework activity.

title: Khan Academy: Displaying and Describing Data
description: Comprehensive video lessons and practice exercises covering data displays, measures of center, and data analysis. Includes immediate feedback and mastery tracking.

title: Learn.Genetics (University of Utah) — Cells Module
description: Genetic Science Learning Center's "Amazing Cells" hub, with multiple interactives covering organelle function, cell scale, and how cells relate to tissues and organs. Includes a 2-week middle school unit.

title: Manitoba Math Connections: Data Management
description: Curriculum-aligned resources for teaching data management concepts including data collection methods, graph selection, and statistical analysis appropriate for middle years students.

title: Marbleslides: Lines
description: Students transform lines so that marbles roll through stars in this engaging game. By adjusting slopes and y-intercepts to guide marbles through targets, students develop deep understanding of how parameters affect linear equations.

title: Match My Parabola + Marble Slides
description: Scaffolded series of challenges where students match given parabolas by manipulating equations in standard, vertex, and factored forms. Develops flexibility with different representations of quadratic functions and builds parameter understanding.

title: Math Antics - Basic Probability
description: Explore vocabulary and concepts related to probability in this YouTube video.

title: Math Playground — Algebraic Reasoning (Balance Equations)
description: Quick-play balance puzzles where students determine the value of shapes by reasoning about what keeps the scale balanced. Useful as a 5-10 minute opener or station, accessible on tablets.

title: Math That Matters 2 (David Stocker / Strong Nations) — Social Justice Math, Grades 6–9
description: Toronto teacher David Stocker's collection of 50 social-justice math lessons for grades 6–9; multiple lessons explicitly engage Indigenous land, water, and reconciliation themes — ideal for Grade 7–8 data-literacy units.

title: Mint: Budgeting Basics for Beginners
description: Learn budgeting fundamentals with interactive tools and real-world examples. Students create sample budgets and track hypothetical expenses to build money management skills.

title: Money and Youth — CFEE Free Textbook (15 Modules)
description: Canadian Foundation for Economic Education's free 15-module textbook on personal finance basics, budgeting, borrowing, saving, and goal setting — a comprehensive teacher-and-student reference covering the full F1 strand.

title: MoneyTime Kids - Financial Literacy Online
description: Free financial literacy resources for teachers, parents and children.

title: Moving in the Plane (IM 8.1.1)
description: An Illustrative Mathematics activity where students explore the relationship between translations, rotations, and reflections. Students investigate how transformations preserve distance and angle measures, building toward understanding congruence.

title: Mystery Science — Chemical Reactions and Properties of Matter
description: Mystery Science's chemistry unit features open-and-go lessons in which students dissolve everyday chemicals to make solutions and explore the particulate nature of matter. Strong lab-style hooks and real-world phenomena.

title: NASA JPL — Learn About the Universe with the James Webb Space Telescope
description: JPL's curated teaching package on JWST, including grade-appropriate lessons on angular size, cosmological redshift, and the electromagnetic spectrum — directly aligned to SNC1W's universe-study strand.

title: NCTR — Residential School History Teaching Resources
description: Specialized NCTR sub-collection on residential school history, with age-appropriate framing aligned to the TRC Calls to Action and the Ontario Strand A focus on the Indian Act and assimilation policies.

title: NRICH — Geometry and Measurement for Secondary
publisher: Curated Collections & Enrichment (NRICH, Open Middle, Youcubed, Fishtank, CEMC)
description: Cambridge's NRICH project offers free, rich mathematical tasks for secondary students exploring measurement concepts through investigation and problem-solving.

title: NRICH — Number Operations and Calculation Methods (Age 11–16)
description: University of Cambridge NRICH problem set on operations and calculation methods, suitable for enrichment, warm-ups, or Building Thinking Classrooms-style task work in Grades 7-8.

title: NRICH — Patterns and Sequences (Age 11–14)
description: University of Cambridge NRICH project's age 11–14 patterns and sequences collection — open-ended problems and investigations suitable for enrichment, extension, or whole-class problem-solving.

title: NRICH — Year 7 Working Systematically
description: NRICH Year 7 problem set that builds the computational-thinking habits of mind Grade 7 C3 assumes — systematic enumeration, variation, and procedural reasoning.

title: Ontario Teachers' Federation - Problem-Based Instruction Resources
description: OTF resources on problem-based instruction approaches that help students see connections between mathematical strands through structured problem-solving.

title: OpenSciEd — 7.5 Ecosystem Dynamics
description: OpenSciEd's free Grade 7 Ecosystem Dynamics unit (NGSS MS-LS2-1, MS-LS2-2, MS-LS2-4, MS-LS2-5, MS-ESS3-3). High-quality OER unit adaptable to Ontario B-strand expectations.

title: Opensource.com — How I Get Students Excited About Math with Python and Raspberry Pi
description: Practising teacher's walkthrough of how they scaffold Python coding alongside middle-school math, including Raspberry-Pi-free browser-based alternatives.

title: Our First Home: Understanding Housing Costs
description: Introduction to housing costs and what it takes to save for a first home. Students explore mortgages, down payments, and the true cost of homeownership in Canada.

title: Our World in Data — Economic Inequality
publisher: Global data and visualization tools (HDI / GDP / SDGs)
description: University of Oxford project producing readable, evidence-based briefings on global inequality, accompanied by interactive graphs that can be embedded in student work or used as classroom visuals.

title: Our World in Data — Urbanization
description: Open-access research entry covering global urbanization with downloadable data and embeddable interactive charts on urban share, megacities, and rural-urban migration — strong source for student data inquiry.

title: Our World in Data: Global Data Explorer
description: Interactive charts and maps showing global trends in health, population, and economics. Students explore real data while developing skills in reading and interpreting complex visualizations.

title: Past the Potholes — Scratch Coding Lessons for Intermediate (Grades 7-8)
description: Teacher-created structured progression through Scratch for intermediate students; includes video tutorials, practice activities, and progressively complex projects suitable for Grade 7 C3 expectations.

title: Poetry Foundation: Poetry for Students
description: Curated poetry collections with teaching guides, audio recordings, and analysis tools. Introduces students to diverse poets and poetic forms.

title: Polygraph: Graphs
description: In this paired activity, one student chooses a graph and the other asks yes/no questions to identify it. Students develop vocabulary and strategy while exploring key features of linear functions and graphical representations.

title: Polypad (Amplify) — Virtual Manipulatives
description: Free virtual manipulatives platform for exploring patterns, equations, and algebraic thinking through interactive digital tools; includes pre-built activities and customizable templates.

title: Polypad by Mathigon
description: Virtual manipulatives with built-in counters, variables, and coordinate grids — students can build growing patterns and then translate them into Scratch code, connecting C1 (patterns) and C3 (coding).

title: Polypad – Mean of a Data Set (Lesson)
description: A guided Polypad lesson where students manipulate data points to explore the concept of mean and how it responds to extreme values. Useful for building intuition about measures of centre.

title: Polypad: Probability Manipulatives
description: Virtual manipulatives for exploring probability including dice, coins, spinners, and cards. Students can conduct experiments and collect data in an intuitive drag-and-drop interface.

title: Population Education — People and the Planet: Lessons for a Sustainable Future
publisher: Sustainable Cities, Population, and Lesson Plans
description: Population Education middle-school curriculum unit with lessons on population density, urban land use, sustainability, and environmental decision-making — strong support for B1 sustainable-settlement and B3 settlement-trends expectations.

title: Practical Money Skills Canada — Lesson Plans
description: Practical Money Skills Canada's "Choices & Decisions" curriculum: free lesson plans on setting financial goals, building personal budgets, and using credit and banking services — aligned to Canadian classrooms.

title: Project Learning Tree Canada — Education Resources
description: Initiative of the Sustainable Forestry Initiative offering K-8 activity guides ("Explore Your Environment") that integrate forest literacy, social studies, and SDG-aligned investigations.

title: Rainbow District School Board — De-streaming Grade 9
publisher: Problem-Based Learning, Enrichment, and Equity Resources
description: Rainbow DSB's public information page on the rationale and structure of de-streaming Grade 9, with parent/guardian-facing language, FAQ, and links to support resources — a useful equity and policy reference for understanding the *why* of MTH1W.

title: Royal Astronomical Society of Canada — Education and Public Outreach
description: Canada's leading astronomy organization (founded 1868) provides curriculum aids, classroom support materials, and "Discover the Universe" content. Connects students with local RASC centres for outreach star nights tied to Strand E.

title: SaskMATH Resources for Mathematics Indigenous Ways of Knowing (Resource Bank)
publisher: SaskMath — Indigenous Ways of Knowing in Mathematics
description: Resource Bank's curated mirror of the SaskMath Indigenous Ways collection — useful when a school librarian or coach needs a single shareable link with rich descriptors.

title: Science North — Grade 7 Lesson Plans
description: Ontario-aligned Grade 7 lessons from Science North in Sudbury covering structural strength and stability, with hands-on activities and curriculum-linked handouts.

title: Science North — Grade 8 Lesson Plans
description: Ontario-curriculum-aligned Grade 8 lessons from Science North supporting all four strands, with cross-strand investigations and inquiry activities.

title: Smarter Balanced — Stack of Cups Grade 8 Performance Task
description: A high-quality released Smarter Balanced Grade 8 performance task with a four-point rubric. Adaptable to Ontario Grade 8 Algebra C1/C2 and Measurement E2 — students model height vs. number of cups and justify their reasoning.

title: SolveMe Mobiles — Balance Puzzles for Algebraic Reasoning
description: Hanging-mobile puzzles that translate naturally into algebraic moves: balancing a mobile is mathematically equivalent to solving a system of equations. Excellent low-floor entry to equation reasoning that visibly transfers to symbolic work.

title: SplashLearn: Probability Games
description: Engaging probability games with colorful animations and instant feedback. Covers likely/unlikely events, comparing probabilities, and simple probability calculations.

title: State of Ontario's Biodiversity — Biodiversity in Ontario's Elementary and Secondary School Curricula
description: Ontario Biodiversity Council overview of where biodiversity, habitat loss, invasive/endangered species, and ecosystem expectations appear across the curriculum. Useful for cross-strand connections and long-range planning.

title: Statistics Canada — 2021 Census Geography Maps
description: Official Statistics Canada portal of 2021 Census reference and thematic maps, including population centres, dissemination areas, and urban/rural classifications — the authoritative Canadian dataset for settlement-pattern analysis.

title: Statistics Canada: Census at School
description: Students participate in a national survey, then analyze real aggregated data from students across Canada. Teaches survey design, data collection, and comparative analysis skills.

title: Storyboard That: Visual Storytelling
description: Digital storyboard creator for visual storytelling, plot mapping, and character analysis. Students create comic-style narratives to demonstrate comprehension.

title: Teacher Resource Cabin — Grade 8 Number Workbook (Ontario Math)
description: Ontario-aligned Grade 8 Strand B workbook with explanation worksheets, practice sheets, quiz, and culminating unit test covering Number Sets, Integer Operations, Powers/Scientific Notation, Square Roots, Fractions, Decimals/Percentages, and Rates/Ratios.

title: Teacher Resource Cabin — New France and British North America Workbook
description: Editable Grade 7 student workbook covering every major topic in Strand A, designed for use as a stand-alone unit or as differentiated/remedial support material.

title: Teachers Pay Teachers — FREE Grade 7 Ontario Math Coding Worksheets (PDF + Google Slides)
publisher: Teaching Units & Lesson Plans — TPT & Commercial
description: Free practice and consolidation worksheets for every Grade 7 Ontario C3 expectation; complements the free digital slides unit but can be used standalone.

title: Teachers Pay Teachers — Grade 8 Ontario Math: Algebraic Expressions, Equations, Inequalities, Monomials
publisher: Teaching Units from TPT and Commercial
description: Comprehensive Grade 8 Ontario Math unit covering C2 monomials, algebraic expressions, multi-step equations (including variables on both sides), and inequalities — digital slides with interactive activities.

title: The Atlas of Canada — Toporama Interactive Topographic Maps
description: Free public access to Canada's National Topographic System with layered relief, drainage, forest cover, and transportation features — ideal for student inquiry projects on physical patterns.

title: The Canadian Encyclopedia — Resources for Grade 8: History
description: Bilingual Historica Canada hub linking Grade 8 strand expectations directly to encyclopedia articles on Confederation, Louis Riel, the Indian Act, treaties, and Black history — a single-stop reference for student inquiry.

title: The Learning Exchange: Money Matters
description: Ontario-based resources for teaching financial literacy, including lesson plans, student activities, and assessment tools aligned with the Ontario curriculum.

title: TIPS4RM (Targeted Implementation & Planning Supports for Revised Mathematics) - Grade 7
description: Ministry-developed three-part lesson plans for Grade 7 covering cross-strand topics including describing patterns, data analysis, composite figures, and transformations.

title: TIPS4RM — Continuum and Connections: Perimeter, Area, and Volume (Grades 6–10)
publisher: Official Curriculum Documents and Ministry Supports
description: A Ministry-supported TIPS4RM (Targeted Implementation and Planning Supports) document mapping the conceptual progression of perimeter, area, and volume across Grades 6–10. Includes diagnostic tasks and lesson supports for Grade 7–8 measurement.

title: Toronto Region Conservation Authority — Wetland Ecosystems Program
description: TRCA's Grade 7 wetland ecosystems program focusing on Ontario wetlands: types, importance, historical destruction, biodiversity, invertebrates, indicator and alien species. Booked field trip or in-class delivery.

title: Transformation Golf: Non-Rigid Motion
description: An extension activity including translations, reflections, rotations, and dilations. Students explore the difference between rigid and non-rigid transformations while solving geometric puzzles that strengthen spatial reasoning.

title: Transformation Golf: Rigid Motion
description: A 30-45 minute activity where students use translations, reflections, and rotations to transform pre-images onto target images. Students apply existing knowledge of transformations to complete a series of geometric challenges with immediate visual feedback.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activity 13: Une visite au zoo
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students learn the names of animals found in a zoo, organized by pavilion (Africa, the Americas, and Canada), and check their understanding with a matching game.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activity 15: Explorons la vieille ville de Québec
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the francophone Québec City, discovering notable places to visit such as the Château Frontenac and popular leisure activities.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activity 19: L’hiver
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the winter season and learn vocabulary for winter clothing such as a tuque, mittens, boots, and a coat through a video and activities.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activity 5: Où j’habite
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the different types of buildings people live in, along with rooms of the home and the activities done in each one.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activity 7: Le Carnaval de Québec : partie 1
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the Carnaval de Québec celebration, meeting its mascot Bonhomme and learning vocabulary for activities you can do at the carnival.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 Science and Technology (Global Focus) Learning Activity 10: Sustainable Manufacturing
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 Science and Technology Learning Activities
description: Looks at fashion as a global process and the series of steps used to manufacture clothing, with attention to sustainability.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 5 Science and Technology (Ontario Focus) Learning Activity 7: The Power of Water
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 5 Science and Technology Learning Activities
description: Uses a poem about natural wonders and a video on waterfalls to explore the power and movement of water.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activity 13: Les invitations : partie 1
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the concept of parties and celebrations and learn the French vocabulary used to describe different kinds of fêtes.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activity 1: Une journée au parc d'attractions
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore an amusement park map and learn French vocabulary for attractions and treats by matching rides and snacks to their labels.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activity 21: L'alimentation : partie 1
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore a video about healthy snacks and learn French vocabulary related to food and snacking.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activity 6: Protégeons l'environnement
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activities
description: Centered on Earth Day (April 22), students explore the importance of protecting the environment and learn related French vocabulary.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activity 8: Le cinéma et les critiques de film
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 7 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the history of cinema via the video « Top 5 sur le cinéma » and learn French vocabulary for talking about and reviewing films.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 7 Geography Natural Resources, Learning Activity 8
description: Capstone learning activity guiding students through an inquiry-based investigation of a chosen natural resource issue and the development of an action plan.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 11: Honorons les racines de nos sports
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students survey a variety of sports with Indigenous origins and take a closer look at one in particular: lacrosse.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 13: J'adore les films
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the world of film, learning cinema vocabulary, film genres, and how to read and write favourable or unfavourable movie reviews.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 16: Le cinéma
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the history of cinema, reviewing film vocabulary and genres and considering which film-industry jobs interest them.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 17: Les Jeux paralympiques
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the Paralympic Games, which allow athletes with physical disabilities to achieve great sporting performances on the international stage, supported by a video and vocabulary.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 19: L'article de journal
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the newspaper article and francophonie in their communities, learning journalism terms and analyzing a photo for information.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 1: Pourquoi apprendre le français?
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the importance of learning French, reviewing a brochure on reasons to continue French studies and testing their comprehension with true/false activities.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 24: Les conversations formelles et informelles
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore and participate in conversations, focusing on the distinction between formal and informal conversations and the use of 'tu' versus 'vous.'

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 6: La diversité au Canada
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the importance of diversity in Canada through a video about Michaëlle Jean and prepare to interview someone not born in Canada about their experience.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 8: Peux-tu lire les horaires?
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore a Quebec school timetable, practising vocabulary for schedules and school subjects through matching and sorting activities.

title: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activity 9: Les transitions à l'école
publisher: TVO Learn — Grade 8 FSL Learning Activities
description: Students explore the transition from Grade 8 to secondary school, working with vocabulary about schedules, subjects, and school staff roles.

title: Two-Eyed Seeing — Place-Based Learning Resource
description: Etuaptmumk/Two-Eyed Seeing framework from Mi'kmaw Elder Albert Marshall — supports teachers in pairing Western geography concepts (climate zones, landforms) with Indigenous knowledges of place.

title: UCDSB LibGuide — Geometry and Spatial Sense, Grade 7
description: Upper Canada District School Board's curated library guide collecting vetted geometry and spatial sense resources for Grade 7, including links to interactive tools, videos, and practice activities.

title: UCDSB VLC — Grade 8 Geography: Global Settlement: Patterns and Sustainability
description: Upper Canada DSB bilingual LibGuide for Strand B with categorized links to atlases, statistics portals, video, and student inquiry support, useful for both English and French Immersion classrooms.

title: UNDP — Human Development Reports Data Center
publisher: Global data and visualization tools (HDI / GDP / SDGs)
description: Official UNDP data center hosting the Human Development Index, Gender Inequality Index, and country-level profiles. Source data for any Strand C choropleth or comparison assignment.

title: UNICEF Canada — Resources for Teachers
description: UNICEF Canada's K–8 Global Classroom resources cover children's rights, global poverty, and humanitarian issues — Canadian-context lessons that align with the strand's quality-of-life expectations.

title: Upper Canada VLC — Grade 8 Geography: Global Inequalities (LibGuide)
publisher: Ontario-aligned curriculum platforms (TVO Learn + VLC libguides)
description: Upper Canada DSB virtual library guide that organizes vetted databases, e-books and current articles around the Global Inequalities strand questions (How do we measure development? What biases exist?).

title: Virtual Nerd: Probability Video Tutorials
description: Step-by-step video explanations of probability concepts from basic to advanced. Each video includes examples and practice problems with detailed solutions.

title: Wacky Number Songs - Probability
description: Let's play the probability game! Probability is the chance of an event occurring - certain, likely, unlikely, or impossible. For example, it will be impossible not to tap your feet during Ava's song about probability!

title: Waterloo Catholic DSB — Grade 7 History Virtual Learning Commons
description: Waterloo Catholic DSB's curriculum-aligned guide for Grade 7 History; one-stop landing page with primary sources, video archives, and curated databases for the New France/BNA strand.

title: WCDSB VLC — Grade 8 Geography: Global Settlement Patterns and Sustainability LibGuide
description: Waterloo Catholic District School Board Virtual Learning Commons LibGuide curating board-licensed databases, eBooks, atlases, and streaming video aligned to Grade 8 Geography Strand B sub-strands.

title: Whose Land — Interactive Territorial Map with Lesson Plans
description: Indigenous-led interactive map of territories and treaties with Ontario-aligned lesson plans. Math-rich extensions include scale drawing, distance calculations, and population data analysis for Grade 7–8.

title: World Bank — Poverty and Inequality Platform
publisher: Global data and visualization tools (HDI / GDP / SDGs)
description: Tracks global poverty headcount and the Gini coefficient by country and over time; useful for inquiry into which regions face the deepest inequality and why.

title: YouCubed (Stanford) — Number Sense Activities
description: YouCubed's number sense resource collection from Stanford University promotes flexible mathematical thinking through engaging tasks. Activities develop students' ability to interact with numbers conceptually and flexibly, moving beyond rote procedural approaches to deep number understanding.

title: Youcubed — Visual Math Activities & Problem Tasks (Jo Boaler)
description: Research-based activities from Stanford's Youcubed center, led by Jo Boaler. Emphasizes growth mindset and visual problem-solving. Includes free downloadable task collections.

title: Your Money: A Guide for Students
description: Free resource from Canadian Bankers Association covering banking basics, opening your first account, understanding credit and debit, and protecting yourself from fraud.
